questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "She can play the piano beautifully, ___"
questions.answer: but she couldn't read music when she started.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "After hours of trying, he managed to ___"
questions.answer: fix the computer before the presentation.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "I wasn't able to sleep last night ___"
questions.answer: because it was so hot in the room.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "They succeeded in finishing ___"
questions.answer: the project on time despite all the problems.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "Are you able to drive? ___"
questions.answer: Yes, but I couldn't drive at all two years ago.

questions.prompt: My little sister _____ (can) already read and she is only four years old.
questions.answer: can

questions.prompt: We _____ (manage to) get tickets for the concert even though it was sold out.
questions.answer: managed to

questions.prompt: He _____ (could) run very fast when he was at school, but not anymore.
questions.answer: could

questions.prompt: After a lot of hard work, she _____ (succeed in) getting the job she wanted.
questions.answer: succeeded in

questions.prompt: I'm not _____ (be able to) come to the meeting on Monday — I have another appointment.
questions.answer: able to

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "He studied really hard for months and then he succeeded to pass all his exams."
questions.answer: 'Succeed' is always followed by 'in' + -ing form: 'he succeeded in passing all his exams.'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "When she was a child, she can swim across the whole pool."
questions.answer: For past abilities, use 'could': 'she could swim across the whole pool.'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "After three hours of hard work, they finally managed to completing the task."
questions.answer: 'Manage to' is followed by the base verb, not -ing: 'they managed to complete the task.'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "My grandfather could speaks five languages and he travelled all over the world."
questions.answer: After 'could', use the base form: 'My grandfather could speak five languages.'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "We worked on it all afternoon but in the end we weren't able finish it before dinner."
questions.answer: 'Be able to' needs 'to' before the verb: 'we weren't able to finish it.'
She managed to fix the problem.
He could run really fast when he was young.
They succeeded in winning the competition.
Can you speak any other languages?
I wasn't able to finish the test in time.
